title: wurmbea dioicea
intro: Wurmbea dioica, also known as the Early Nancy or Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. It is believed to have medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Wurmbea dioica include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with colds and flu. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through drying and infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to make a tea, or by incorporating it into ointments and salves for topical use.
